LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT THOMAS WERDEL'S MOTION TO TRANSFER CASE TO THE PROBATE DEPARTMENT IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO THE PROBATE DEPARTMENT FOR ALL PURPOSES. PROBATE CODE 17000(A) PROVIDES THAT THE PROBATE DEPARTMENT HAS "EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF PROCEEDINGS CONCERNING THE INTERNAL AFFAIRS OF TRUSTS." ALL OF THE ALLEGATIONS AGAINST THOMAS WERDEL CONCERN THE INTERNAL AFFAIRS OF THE ALBERTA W. CHANDLER TRUST. FURTHER, PROBATE CODE 17000(B) PROVIDES THAT THE PROBATE DEPARTMENT HAS CONCURRENT JURISDICTION OVER "OTHER ACTIONS AND PROCEEDINGS INVOLVING TRUSTEES AND THIRD PERSONS." ACCORDINGLY, THE ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENDANT BRUCE CHANDLER INTERFERED WITH THE ADMINISTRATION OF THE TRUST MAY ALSO BE RESOLVED BY THE PROBATE DEPARTMENT. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. CCP 396B(B) PERTAINS TO A CHANGE OF VENUE, NOT TO A CHANGE OF DEPARTMENTS WITHIN A SUPERIOR COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Thomas Werdel To Transfer Case To The Probate Department
Off Calendar Jun-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gregory L. Young, Haney & Young LLP, Tel. 213.228.6500, for plaintiffs;
Stuart M. Gordon, of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel. 415.986.5900, for defendants Thomas Werdel;
Michael S. Brophy, of Russ August & Kabat, Tel. 310.826.7474, for defendants Bruce Chandler.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THOMAS WERDEL'S Motion To Transfer Case To The Probate Department is granted. The case is transferred to the Probate Department for all purposes. Probate Code 17000(a) provides that the Probate Department has "exclusive jurisdiction of proceedings concerning the internal affairs of trusts." All of the allegations against Thomas Werdel concern the internal affairs of the Alberta W. Chandler Trust. Further, Probate Code 17000(b) provides that the Probate Department has concurrent jurisdiction over "other actions and proceedings involving trustees and third persons." Accordingly, the allegations that Defendant Bruce Chandler interfered with the administration of the trust may also be resolved by the Probate Department. The request for sanctions is denied. CCP 396b(b) pertains to a change of venue, not to a change of departments within a superior court. Order signed in open court. Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/HK)
